highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.126 Released
highlights.summary: The latest release adds a smarter `/model` picker that pulls available models directly from your gateway's endpoint when using a custom `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, making it easier to switch models in enterprise setups. A new `claude project purge [path]` command lets you cleanly wipe all Claude Code state for a project — including transcripts, tasks, and file history — which is handy when starting fresh. Update via `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` to get these improvements.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.121 Released
highlights.summary: This release introduces an `alwaysLoad` option for MCP server configs, ensuring specific tools are always available without waiting for tool-search deferral — great for frequently used MCP integrations. A new `claude plugin prune` command removes orphaned plugin dependencies, keeping your setup clean. There's also a type-to-filter improvement for faster navigation inside Claude Code's UI.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.119 Released
highlights.summary: Settings configured via `/config` — such as theme, editor mode, and verbosity — now persist to `~/.claude/settings.json` and respect the full project/local/policy override hierarchy, so your preferences survive restarts. A new `prUrlTemplate` setting lets teams point the footer PR badge at a custom code-review URL instead of github.com, useful for self-hosted GitLab or Gerrit setups. This is a quality-of-life release worth updating to if you rely on persistent preferences.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus family, now available for use with Claude Code. If you're tackling complex, multi-step coding tasks or large refactors, switching to Opus 4.7 via the `/model` picker may deliver noticeably better results. Check the Anthropic blog for pricing and availability details before switching in production workflows.

highlights.title: Full Claude Code Tutorial: It's Easier than You Think (no coding)
highlights.summary: This beginner-focused YouTube tutorial walks you through using Claude Code from scratch — no prior coding experience required. It covers installation, basic prompting, and how to get Claude Code to complete real tasks in VS Code. A great starting point if you've been intimidated by the terminal-based workflow.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This compact YouTube tutorial aims to take you from zero to productive with Claude Code in just 30 minutes, covering core workflows, useful commands, and practical tips. It's ideal for developers who learn best by watching a fast-paced overview rather than reading documentation. Bookmark it as a quick refresher when you want to level up your Claude Code habits.

highlights.title: Ask HN: How are you using AI code assistants on large messy legacy code bases?
highlights.summary: This Hacker News thread gathers real-world experiences from seasoned developers using Claude and other AI coding tools on large, complex legacy codebases — the kind where AI often struggles. Developers share practical strategies like scoping Claude to single modules, generating tests first, and using CLAUDE.md to give context about the codebase. If you work on inherited or tangled code, the community's hard-won tips here are worth reading.

highlights.title: EvanFlow – A TDD-Driven Feedback Loop for Claude Code
highlights.summary: EvanFlow is an open-source tool on GitHub that wraps Claude Code in a test-driven development feedback loop, automatically running tests after each AI-generated change and feeding results back to Claude for correction. This agentic loop can significantly reduce the manual back-and-forth when building features with Claude Code. Developers interested in more autonomous, reliable code generation should check out the repo and try integrating it into their CI workflow.

tips_of_the_day: Run `claude project purge .` when a project's Claude Code state feels stale or corrupted — it wipes transcripts and config entries so you start with a clean slate without touching your actual source files.
Set `alwaysLoad: true` on your most-used MCP server in your config file so its tools are instantly available every session, skipping the tool-search delay that can slow down agentic workflows.

Before handing Claude Code a large legacy file, paste a short comment block at the top describing what the module does and what NOT to change — this acts like a mini CLAUDE.md and dramatically reduces unwanted rewrites.
